text: ["Machine Learning automates analytical model building using algorithms that learn from data to recognize patterns and make decisions, including supervised learning (using labeled data), unsupervised learning (exploring unknown structures), and multivariate analysis (MVA) methods for classification and regression tasks.", "Neural networks are inspired by the human brain, using interconnected artificial neurons that collect and sum inputs, then fire based on activation functions; perceptrons produce binary output while sigmoid provides smoother gradients for learning, with common activations including linear, step, ReLU, sigmoid, tanh, and softmax, organized into input, hidden, and output layers.", "Training uses backpropagation to update weights by comparing outputs to actual values, with gradient descent (w(t+1) = w(t) - η(∂L/∂w)) minimizing loss; learning rate too small traps in local minima, too large jumps out of global minimum, and deep networks with 2+ hidden layers build hierarchical concepts while ReLU is now default but sigmoid/tanh can cause vanishing gradients.", "Regularization techniques including L1 (making weights zero), L2 (weight decay), dropout (random neuron removal), early stopping, and data augmentation combat overfitting, which occurs when networks learn training data accurately but fail to generalize; training data is split into training, validation, and test sets."]
json_data: Machine Learning automates analytical model building using algorithms that learn from data to recognize patterns and make decisions, including supervised learning (using labeled data), unsupervised learning (exploring unknown structures), and multivariate analysis (MVA) methods for classification and regression tasks.

Neural networks are inspired by the human brain, using interconnected artificial neurons that collect and sum inputs, then fire based on activation functions; perceptrons produce binary output while sigmoid provides smoother gradients for learning, with common activations including linear, step, ReLU, sigmoid, tanh, and softmax, organized into input, hidden, and output layers.

Training uses backpropagation to update weights by comparing outputs to actual values, with gradient descent (w(t+1) = w(t) - η(∂L/∂w)) minimizing loss; learning rate too small traps in local minima, too large jumps out of global minimum, and deep networks with 2+ hidden layers build hierarchical concepts while ReLU is now default but sigmoid/tanh can cause vanishing gradients.

Regularization techniques including L1 (making weights zero), L2 (weight decay), dropout (random neuron removal), early stopping, and data augmentation combat overfitting, which occurs when networks learn training data accurately but fail to generalize; training data is split into training, validation, and test sets.
